Dear Legal Divisj-on:
This firrn serves as the City Attorney for the city of
West Covina. This letter requests your advice on behalf of
Steve Herfert, West Covina City Councilmember.
Councilmember Herfert is 
an employee of 
Southern
California Edison ( ItEdisonrr) earning over $250 a year. 
He
also participates in Edison's profit sharing plan from which
he receives over $t,ooo per year. 
Edison is a regulated
public utility 
listed on the New York Stock Exchange and a
Fortune 500 company as described in 2 CaI. Code of Regs.
1,87 O22 (a) .
The 583-acre BKK landfill 
is located totally within West
Covina. It is owned and operated by the BKK Corporation.
From L972 through November 30, L984, the landfiI1 accepted
hazardous materials as a Class I disposal site. 
The
landfill's 
acceptance of such materials and the subsequent
closure of the class r disposal area have been and continue
to be regulated by the state Department of Health services,
other State agencies and Federal Environmental protection
Agency and not by the city. 
The city has no authority over
the Class I area.
GARVIN

However, BKK al-so operates a Class III 
(househol-d sol-id
waste) disposal area. That use contj,nues pursuant to a city
conditional use permit (ttcuPtt) as $/ell as state regulation.
The City serves as the Local Enforcement Agent (trLEArt) for
the class III 
operation, subject to the approval of the
California Integrated Waste Management Board. The LEA nay
grant or amend a class III 
landfill 
permit only after the
Board's concurrence. The City Council may sit as a hearing
panel to suspend or revoke a permit but also may appoint an
independent panel . 
see Public Resources code S 44001 et.
Egg., and S 44500 et seq. Under the cUP the city nay set the
maximum class IfI landfill- rates. 
The City also may consider
enteringT j.nto other agreenents with BKK as necessary not
related to disposal operations.
The City and BKK have agreed that the Class III 
area
will 
close by November L995, that closure date is not
reguired by state l-aw and could be changed by the City
Council (within the Iinits 
imposed by capacity and state
agencies) . we do not knol^, the financial relationship within
the BKK Corporation bet!'reen the tlro operations, although BKK
operates and is responsible for both. 
We assume, but do not
know, whether any portion of the revenues frorn the Cl-ass fII
operation are used to finance closure costsi we have been
told such costs are financed from electricity 
produced by the
gas turbines which dispose of methane on the site. 
The
electricity 
is sold to Edison at rates and in an amount BKK
has not disclosed.
As a state requirement of closure of the Cl-ass I area,
BKK was required to finance the clean-up and closure of the
site. 
As such a generator, Edison (a1ong with six or seven
others) posted a letter of credit, as described in the letter
from BKK'S ceneral Counsel, a copy of which is attached.
The question which now has arisen 
is 
whether
Council-member Herfert has a potential, conflict of interest on
matters concerning BKK because of Edison,s position as a

ATTORNEYS AT LAM/

guarantor of BKK'S obligation. 
Please let me know if 
you
need additional 
information in order to provide this
analysis. 
My direct t j.ne telephone number is (714) 662-4644.
very truly yours,
RUTAN & TUCKER

Re: BKK Landfill
Dear Betsy:
In response to your inquiry on Southern Caljforn'ia Edison's (Edjson)
participation in an agreement guaranteeing certain BKK Corporation financial
obligat'ions, I can confirm that Edjson is a party to an 0ctober 15, 1987
agreement known as the Participation Agreement.
As you explained, the reason for yourinquiry is to assist you in
determ'ini ng whether an el ected Ci ty Counc j l man that i s cumently empl oyed
by Edison would have a conflict of interest jn voting on any City matters
affecting the BKK Landfill.
I am not at liberty to release a copy of the entire Participation
Agreement, but can d'iscl ose the fol I owi ng 'informat j on:
l. 
To fac'il itate the issuance of a Cal ifornia Po1lution Control Financing
Authority bond in the amount of approxjmately $12.5 mj'llion to be used
to finance the closure of the BKK Landfill Class I disposal area,
Edison and other generators of hazardous waste disposed at the site,
agreed to guarantee repayment of portions of the obligation in the
event of default by BKK Corporation.
2. The Edison portion was $1,280,000.00 and represents its maximum
I'iabif ity under the Aoreement.. No ot.her Iiabjf ity was expressl.v or
impliedly accepted by Edjsorr or any of the other guarantors, althcugh
such di scl aimers may not be affected by prov'is j ons of the state or
federal 1aw applicable to generators of hazardous waste.
3. 
Payment of the bond indebtedness is scheduled to be completed on Ju)y
15, 1995. BKK Corporat jon 'is current 'in its scheduled payments and at
no time since the making of the Agreement has a default occurred.
4. 
Among the affirmative ob1 igations of BKK Corporation under the
Agreement is the obligation to implement the Closure Plan and to obtajn
its Class III landfjll permits. Edjson and the other guarantors reta'in
a right of specific performance.


El i zabeth L.
December 17,
Page 2
Hanna, Esq.

sections.question: Whether Councilmember Herfert has a potential conflict of interest on matters concerning BKK because of Edison's position as a guarantor of BKK's obligation.
sections.analysis:
. 
My direct t j.ne telephone number is (714) 662-4644.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a City Councilmember employed by Southern California Edison have a conflict of interest in voting on matters affecting a landfill operator when Edison has guaranteed portions of the landfill operator's financial obligations?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request to the FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document includes the initial request letter from the City Attorney and an attached letter from BKK Corporation's General Counsel providing factual background. No CONCLUSION or FPPC response is present in this document. OCR quality is moderate with some scanning artifacts visible.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from the City Attorney of West Covina seeking FPPC advice on whether Councilmember Steve Herfert, an Edison employee, has a conflict of interest regarding BKK landfill matters, given Edison's role as a guarantor of BKK's bond obligations. The letter includes background on the landfill operations and Edison's $1,280,000 maximum liability guarantee.
